grades.expectation: Response notes the beneficiary's own extension as relevant to enforceability (acquiescence/waiver argument)
grades.reason: Clearly discusses waiver, abandonment, and acquiescence arguments based on the beneficiary's 2015 extension

grades.expectation: Response discusses whether indemnity insurance is available and the 'no approach' prerequisite
grades.reason: Explicitly mentions indemnity insurance and the key 'no approach' rule in section 6

grades.expectation: Response mentions s84 LPA 1925 ground (aa) — covenant impedes reasonable use of land
grades.reason: Discusses s84 LPA 1925 application and mentions ground that covenant 'impedes some reasonable use of the land'

grades.expectation: Response distinguishes between a front/visible extension and rear extension in terms of risk
grades.reason: No distinction made between front/visible vs rear extensions in terms of risk assessment

grades.expectation: Response provides practical advice on sequencing: decide insurance vs release before any contact
grades.reason: Clearly advises on sequencing - mentions seeking release/consent first, and notes insurance only available if no approach made
